title: Teaching my grandfather to use a video call
summary: Narrate the slow, patient afternoons you spent teaching an older relative to do something new, and what you both learned.
topic: Teaching an older relative a new skill across a generation gap
prompt: Write 9–11 sentences narrating the time you taught an older person a new skill (a video call, a phone, a recipe). Describe the steps, the frustration, and the small success. Use past simple, sequencing words (first, then, after that), and 'so/because'.
hintStarters: My grandfather had never used a phone with a screen, so I…
First I showed him the green button, then I asked him to…
He pressed the wrong thing many times, and at one point he…
When my aunt's face finally appeared on the screen, he…
sampleText: My grandfather had never used a phone with a screen, so one Sunday I sat next to him to teach him a video call. He wanted to see his sister, who lives far away and cannot travel anymore. First I showed him the green button and explained that it starts the call. Then I asked him to press it himself. He pressed the wrong thing many times, and the screen kept going dark. At one point he became frustrated and said the phone was too clever for him. I told him to slow down, and we tried again more slowly. I had to explain each step again, because he forgot the order. After about thirty minutes, he finally pressed the right button at the right time. When his sister's face appeared on the screen, he went completely quiet. They had not seen each other for almost two years. He touched the screen gently, as if he could feel her hand. After the call, he wrote the steps on a piece of paper so he could do it alone next time. That afternoon taught me to be patient, and it taught him that he was not too old to learn. Now he calls her every week, and he is very proud of himself.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chưa bao giờ dùng điện thoại có màn hình

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I sat next to him to teach him a video call
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi ngồi cạnh ông để dạy ông một cuộc gọi video

sampleAnnotations.phrase: First I showed him the green button
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đầu tiên tôi chỉ cho ông cái nút màu xanh

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Then I asked him to press it himself
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: sau đó tôi bảo ông tự nhấn nó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I told him to slow down
I had to explain each step again
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi phải giải thích lại từng bước

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he finally pressed the right button at the right time
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cuối cùng ông nhấn đúng nút vào đúng lúc

sampleAnnotations.phrase: his sister's face appeared on the screen
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: khuôn mặt em gái ông xuất hiện trên màn hình

sampleAnnotations.phrase: They had not seen each other for almost two years
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: họ đã không gặp nhau gần hai năm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: as if he could feel her hand

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he wrote the steps on a piece of paper
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ông viết các bước ra một tờ giấy

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Sequencing words to order the steps
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Bài dùng 'First', 'Then', 'At one point', 'After about thirty minutes' để sắp xếp các bước theo thứ tự. Những từ này giúp người đọc theo dõi câu chuyện dễ hơn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The text uses 'First', 'Then', 'At one point', and 'After about thirty minutes' to order the steps in sequence. These words help the reader follow the story.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for an earlier state
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'They had not seen each other for almost two years' dùng quá khứ hoàn thành vì việc không gặp nhau xảy ra trước khoảnh khắc khuôn mặt xuất hiện trên màn hình.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'They had not seen each other for almost two years' uses past perfect because the not-seeing happened before the moment her face appeared on the screen.

sampleTranslationVi: Ông tôi chưa bao giờ dùng điện thoại có màn hình, nên một Chủ nhật tôi ngồi cạnh ông để dạy ông một cuộc gọi video. Ông muốn gặp em gái mình, người sống ở xa và không thể đi lại được nữa. Đầu tiên tôi chỉ cho ông cái nút màu xanh và giải thích rằng nó bắt đầu cuộc gọi. Sau đó tôi bảo ông tự nhấn nó. Ông nhấn nhầm nhiều lần, và màn hình cứ tối đi. Có lúc ông trở nên bực bội và nói cái điện thoại quá thông minh đối với ông. Tôi bảo ông chậm lại, và chúng tôi thử lại chậm hơn. Tôi phải giải thích lại từng bước, vì ông quên thứ tự. Sau khoảng ba mươi phút, cuối cùng ông nhấn đúng nút vào đúng lúc. Khi khuôn mặt em gái ông xuất hiện trên màn hình, ông im lặng hoàn toàn. Họ đã không gặp nhau gần hai năm. Ông chạm nhẹ vào màn hình, như thể có thể cảm nhận bàn tay bà. Sau cuộc gọi, ông viết các bước ra một tờ giấy để lần sau có thể tự làm. Buổi chiều đó dạy tôi phải kiên nhẫn, và nó dạy ông rằng ông không quá già để học. Giờ ông gọi cho bà mỗi tuần, và ông rất tự hào về bản thân.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence about his lack of experience before that day.
options: My grandfather had never used a phone with a screen.
My grandfather has never used a phone with a screen.
My grandfather had never use a phone with a screen.
My grandfather never had use a phone with a screen.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had never used' fits a state before the past story. Present perfect 'has' shifts to now; 'use' should be 'used'; the last has awkward order.
mcQuestions.hint: The inexperience was true before that Sunday — which past form fits?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that orders the first step naturally.
options: First I showed to him the green button.
First I showed him the green button.
First I show him the green button.
At first I showed him the green button firstly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Showed him the green button' uses two objects correctly. 'Showed to him' adds an unneeded 'to'; 'show' lacks past tense; the last is redundant with two ordering words.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'show' need 'to' before the person here?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence reports the request correctly?
options: Then I asked him press it himself.
Then I asked him that he presses it himself.
Then I asked him to press it himself.
Then I asked to him to press it himself.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Ask someone to do something' uses the infinitive 'to press'. The second drops 'to'; the third uses an awkward 'that' clause; 'asked to him' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'asked him', which form of the verb follows?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that uses 'kept' for a repeated annoyance.
options: The screen kept go dark.
The screen kept to go dark.
The screen keeped going dark.
The screen kept going dark.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Keep + verb-ing' (going) means it happened repeatedly. 'Kept go' and 'to go' are wrong; 'keeped' misspells the past of 'keep'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'keep', do you use 'going' or 'to go'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that says he got annoyed naturally.
options: At one point he became frustrated.
At one point he became frustrating.
At one point he become frustrated.
At one point he became frustrate.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Became frustrated' describes how he felt. 'Frustrating' describes a thing that causes the feeling; 'become' lacks past tense; 'frustrate' is the verb base.
mcQuestions.hint: Did he feel the emotion ('-ed') or cause it ('-ing')?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence about explaining again is best written?
options: I had to explained each step again because he forgot the order.
I had to explain each step again because he forgot the order.
I had to explain each steps again because he forgot the order.
I had explain each step again because he forgot the order.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had to explain' uses the base verb after 'had to'. 'Explained' is wrong after 'to'; 'each steps' is ungrammatical; the last drops 'to'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'had to', which verb form follows?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence about the long separation.
options: They had not saw each other for almost two years.
They have not seen each other for almost two years.
They had not seen each other for almost two years.
They had not seen each other since almost two years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had not seen' with 'for + period' fits the moment in the past story. 'Saw' is wrong; present perfect shifts to now; 'since' needs a start point.
mcQuestions.hint: With a period ('two years') and a past story moment, which form fits?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about the moment of success.
options: He finally pressed the right button on the right time.
He finally press the right button at the right time.
He finally pressed the rightly button at the right time.
He finally pressed the right button at the right time.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'At the right time' uses the correct preposition and 'pressed' is past. 'On the right time' is wrong; 'press' lacks past tense; 'rightly button' misuses an adverb.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition goes with 'the right time'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows purpose for writing the steps?
options: He wrote the steps on paper so he could do it alone next time.
He wrote the steps on paper so that do it alone next time.
He wrote the steps on paper for he could do it alone next time.
He wrote the steps on paper so he can do it alone next time.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'So he could do it' expresses past purpose with 'could'. The second drops the subject; 'for he could' is wrong; 'can' clashes with the past narrative.
mcQuestions.hint: In a past story, is 'could' or 'can' used for purpose?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best closing sentence about his pride.
options: Now he calls her every week, and he is proud of him.
Now he calls her every week, and he is proud of himself.
Now he call her every week, and he is proud of himself.
Now he calls her every week, and he is proud for himself.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Proud of himself' uses the reflexive pronoun and 'calls' agrees with 'he'. 'Of him' is wrong; 'call' lacks -s; 'proud for' is the wrong preposition.
mcQuestions.hint: When the subject and object are the same person, which pronoun do you use?

criticalThinkingQuestion: What is it like to teach a skill to someone much older than you, who knows far more about life? What can the teacher learn from the moment?
